name: upnlp

metadata.name: upnlp
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A package designed to support NLP teaching at graduate level

documentation:
# UP-NLP

The Université Paris NLP package (upnlp) is meant to provide support for teaching Natural Language Processing
(NLP) at graduate level. It targets computational linguists, cognitive scientists
and computer scientists.  It aims to help students to get a smooth introduction to modern 
libraries and tools used nowadays in NLP without getting lost into the gory details
and without requiring insanely heavy computational resources.

Thus the library provides:

* Convenient access to small scale data sets relevant for in-class exercises
* Convenient access to small scale language models relevant for in-class exercises.
* A programming interface designed to be easily comparable to real-world full-scale libraries like Huggingface or OpenAI:
  key notions learned with the interface are easy to transfer to real world use cases.


The best way to discover the library is through its documentation.

classes.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc.list_datasets
documentation:
Returns a list of all dataset identifiers currently in the hub

Returns:
    a list of strings

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc.list_models
documentation:
Returns a list of all pretrained models identifiers currently in the hub

Returns:
    a list of strings

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc.get_local_path
documentation:
Returns the local path for a dataset or a model if it comes from the hub
or the path itself if it is not in the hub

Args:
    path (path) : a path
Returns:
    path

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc._download_dir
documentation:
Downloads a full chunk from UPC nextcloud and puts it in cache if not already cached

Args:
   chunk_name (str): the name of the dataset
KwArgs:
   chunk_type (str): either 'datasets' or 'pretrained'

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc._download_binary_file
documentation:
Downloads a single binary file from the nextcloud

Args:
    hubfilename (str)       : name of the shared file on UPC NextCloud
    destination_path (path) : path where to write the file

methods.name: nlp_datasets.HubUpc._download_text_file
documentation:
Downloads a single text file from the nextcloud
Args:
    hubfilename (str)       : name of the shared file on UPC NextCloud
    destination_path (path) : path where to write the file
This is a client allowing to download datasets and pretrained models from UPC nextcloud

classes.name: nlp_datasets.RandomAccessRawText

methods.name: nlp_datasets.RandomAccessRawText.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    data (list): a list of strings (sentences or paragraphs)

    tokenizer (callable): a tokenizer is a callable mapping a string to a list of integers

KwArgs:
   max_seq_size (int): maximum size of a data sequence. Longer sequences are truncated

Wraps a list of strings (sentences or paragraphs) into a pytorch dataset.
Tokenized sequences that are longer than some size are truncated and split into several data items

functions.name: tokenizers.normalize_text
documentation:
For tokenizers splitting tokens on blank spaces it is relevant to normalize spacing before tokenization.
Case is lowered too.
Args:
    text (str): the text to normalize
Returns:
     the normalized text

functions.name: tokenizers.all_en_inflections
documentation:
Returns the inflections for an English lemma
Args:
    wordform (str): an English lemma
    pos      (str): a part of speech code (n,v,a)
Returns:
    a list of inflected wordforms including the original query lemma

classes.name: tokenizers.AutoTokenizer

methods.name: tokenizers.AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained
documentation:
Loads a pretrained model from directory.

Args:

Returns:
    the pretrained model instance
This is a namespace for easy loading of tokenizers defined from this module.

classes.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    unk(str): string for unknown tokens
    pad(str): string for padding tokens
    eos(str): string for eos token
    bos(str): string for bos token

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.signature
documentation:
Returns an md5 checksum of the vocabulary in this segmenter.
Can be used to test if two segmenters perform the exact same segmentation on the same input

Returns:
    a string

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.from_pretrained
documentation:
Loads the tokenizer from the model directory

Args:
  dirpath (path or string) : path to the tokenizer params dir

Returns:
  a DefaultTokenizer object

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.save_pretrained
documentation:
Saves the tokenizer to model dir.

Args:
  dirpath (path or string) : path to the tokenizer params dir

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.add_tokens
documentation:
Adds a list of tokens to the vocabulary.

Args:
  tokens :  a list of strings to add to the vocabulary

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Splits a string into tokens

Args:
  string : a string to tokenize

Returns:
  a list of strings

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids
documentation:
Maps a list of tokens to integer codes

Args:
  tokens : a list of strings
Returns:
  a list of integers

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.encode
documentation:
Encodes a string into a list of integers

Args:
  string : a text to encode
Returns:
  a list of integers

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.decode
documentation:
Decodes a list of integers into a string

Args:
  ids : a list of integers
Returns:
  a string

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.__call__
documentation: @see the encode method

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.pad_id
documentation: the id of the pad token

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.bos_token
documentation: the string used for the begin of sentence token

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.eos_token
documentation: the string used for the end of sentence token

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.eos_token_id
documentation: the integer used for the end of sentence token

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.vocab_size
documentation: the size of the vocabulary

methods.name: tokenizers.AbstractTokenizer.pad_batch
documentation:
Pads a batch of integers with the pad code

Args:
  batch_codes : a list of lists of integers

Returns:
  a list of lists of integers as a tensor
This is the abstract superclass for the tokenizers in this module

classes.name: tokenizers.PunctuationTokenizer

methods.name: tokenizers.PunctuationTokenizer.__init__
documentation:
Creates a tokenizer with some vocabulary and an unknown token

Args:
  base_vocabulary (list): list of strings
  unk (str): string for unknown tokens
  pad (str): string for padding tokens
  eos (str): string for eos token
  bos (str): string for bos token

methods.name: tokenizers.PunctuationTokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Splits a string into tokens

Args:
  string (str) : a string to tokenize

Returns:
  a list of strings

Punctuation tokenizer that approximates the HuggingFace tokenizer interface.
The tokenizer splits the input using punctuation. One may not be able to recover the original input after segmentation

classes.name: tokenizers.WordNetTokenizer

methods.name: tokenizers.WordNetTokenizer.tokenize
documentation:
Splits a string into tokens

Args:
    string (str) : a string to tokenize
    include_separators (bool): flag controlling whether to include inter-token strings (whitespace most of the time)
    wn_lemmatizer (lemmatizer): A callable returning the wordnet lemma(s) for a given word string. wn.Morphy is an example

Returns:
    a list of strings

methods.name: tokenizers.WordNetTokenizer.add_tokens
documentation:
Adds a list of tokens to the vocabulary.

Args:
    tokens :  a list of strings to add to the vocabulary

This is a tokenizer that tokenizes text by recognizing explicitly the wordnet vocabulary.
It may naturally recognize some multi-word expressions provided a properly normalized input text.
It is possible to configure the tokenizer in a mode such that the exact input can be reconstructed from the output tokens.

modules.name: lm_models
documentation: This file contains vanilla implementations of NNLM, RNNLM and Transformer-based language models

classes.name: lm_models.AutoModel

methods.name: lm_models.AutoModel.from_pretrained
documentation:
Loads a pretrained model from directory.

Args:
    path(path) : path to the model directory (either in the hub or local)

Returns:
    the pretrained model instance
This is a namespace for easy loading of models defined from this module.

classes.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.from_pretrained
documentation:
Loads a model from directory

Args:
    path (path): a path to the model dir file

Returns:
    a language model.

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.save_pretrained
documentation:
Saves the model parameters and hyperparameters to directory

Args:
    path (path): path to the model directory

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.get_device
documentation:
Returns the device on which all the parameters of the model are located

Returns:
    device

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.train_lm
documentation:
Trains a model from scratch.

Args:
    train_loader (dataloader): a dataloader on the train set

    valid_loader (dataloader): a dataloader on the valid set

    epochs (int): the number of training epochs

    outdir (path or string): path to the final model directory

KwArgs:
    outdir (path or string): path to the final model directory. If set to None, no model is automatically saved to disk

    LR (float): the initial learning rate of AdamW

    device (str): the device on which to train the model ('cpu','cuda','mps' ...)

    show_progressbar (bool): whether to show the progressbar when training

Returns
    the model with minimal loss over the epochs

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.validate_lm
documentation:
Computes the loss on the validation dataset

Args:
    valid_loader (dataloader) : dataloader for the validation set

KwArgs:
    perplexity (float): if true returns the perplexity of the language model on the validation set, otherwise an averaged loglikelihood

    show_progressbar (bool): whether to show the progressbar when training

Returns :
    float. the averaged validation loss

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel._predict
documentation:
Generates the next token given the prefix.
Args:
    prefix       (list): a list of token ids, the prefix symbol sequence
KwArgs:
    temperature (float): the temperature of the softmax
    do_sample    (bool): whether to use sampling or argmax to generate next token
Returns:
    int. the next token id

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.generate
documentation:
Generates symbols with the language model. This is a generic and inefficient method suitable for generating small texts with small models.
Most larger scale models subclasses will want to override it.

Args:
    prefix       (list): a list of token ids, the prefix symbol sequence

KwArgs:
    temperature (float): the temperature of the softmax
    do_sample    (bool): whether to use sampling or argmax to generate next token
    max_tokens   (int) : the max number of generated tokens
    eos_token_id (int) : generation stops once this token_id is generated

Returns:
    LongTensor. Tensor with the full encoded text

methods.name: lm_models.PytorchLanguageModel.logprobs
documentation:
Computes the logprob given context for each token given in the sequence.
The method prepends a <bos> token at the beginning of the sequence if there is none
Args:
    sequence (list) : a list of integer codes
    bos_id    (int) : code for the bos token
Returns:
    tensor. a tensor of log probabilities of the same size as the input tensor.
    One value for each input token. No value for the bos token

This is an abstract class providing the generic interface to language models: train, eval and generate
Actual model architectures are specified as subclasses.

methods.name: lm_models.LstmLM.__init__
documentation:
An LSTM language model.
Args:
    vocab_size (int) : size of the vocabulary

    emb_size   (int) : size of the embeddings

    hidden_size (int): size of the hidden layer
KwArgs:
    nlayers     (int): number of layers

    pad_value   (int): pad token id

    dropout   (float): value of the dropout

methods.name: lm_models.LstmLM.from_pretrained
documentation:
Loads a model from directory
Args:
    path (path): a path to the model dir file
KwArgs:
    device (device): device where to load the model
Returns:
    a language model.

methods.name: lm_models.LstmLM.save_pretrained
documentation:
Saves the model parameters and hyperparameters to directory
Args:
    path (path): path to the model directory

methods.name: lm_models.LstmLM.forward
documentation:
This takes a batch of N sequences of S tokens each.
For each token it redicts its successors' scores

Args:
    xinputs (tensor): the tokens ids, a tensor of shape (N,S)

Returns:
    the scores. A tensor of shape (N,S,V)

methods.name: lm_models.MarkovianLM.forward
documentation:
This takes a batch of N sequences of S tokens each
And for each token predicts its successors' scores
Args:
   xinputs (tensor): the tokens ids, a tensor of shape (N,S)
Returns:
   the scores. A tensor of shape (N,S,V)
